name: Cerebral folate deficiency phenotype
description: Subset of autism with documented blockade of folate transport into the brain via FOLR1 autoantibodies. High response rate to high-dose folinic acid (leucovorin) per Frye 2018 RCT.
diagnostic_markers: FOLR1 autoantibodies positive; CSF 5-MTHF low; sometimes responsive to folinic acid challenge.

name: Mitochondrial dysfunction phenotype
description: Subset with elevated mitochondrial biomarkers; responds to mitochondrial cocktails (CoQ10, L-carnitine, B vitamins).
diagnostic_markers: Elevated lactate, pyruvate, alanine; muscle biopsy abnormal; ATP production reduced.
prevalence_estimate: ~30-50% show mitochondrial markers; ~5% meet strict criteria

name: Regressive immune-inflammatory phenotype
description: Children who develop typically until 18-36 months then regress. Elevated inflammatory markers; family history of autoimmunity common.
diagnostic_markers: Loss of milestones at 18-36 months; elevated cytokines; family hx autoimmunity.

name: Autism + GI / microbiome phenotype
description: Subset with chronic GI symptoms and altered gut microbiome. FMT trials show sustained improvement (Kang 2019).
diagnostic_markers: Chronic GI symptoms; altered microbiome; food sensitivities.

name: mTOR pathway syndromic (TSC, PTEN)
description: Single-gene variants in mTOR pathway. Rapamycin/sirolimus FDA-approved for TSC; off-label use in autism + mTOR variants.
diagnostic_markers: TSC1/TSC2 or PTEN variants; tubers on imaging; macrocephaly.

name: Fragile X (FMR1)
description: Single-gene cause. Multiple failed phase 3 trials; mechanism continues to refine.
prevalence_estimate: ~1-2% of autism cases (~50% of Fragile X has autism)

name: GABA/Cl- imbalance phenotype
description: Phenotype defined by responsiveness to bumetanide; may reflect persistence of immature GABA polarity.
